title: microseris douglasii subsp. tenella
intro: Microseris douglasii subsp. tenella, also known as Douglas' silverpuff or Douglas' silver daisy, is a rare and endangered annual plant species native to the Pacific coastal regions of North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which make it effective in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Microseris douglasii subsp. tenella include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers are often dried and used to make teas or infusions that can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
